name: States of Matter Transitions
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Solid phase"]
    N2{"Heat or cool?"}
    N3["Melt to liquid"]
    N4["Liquid phase"]
    N5["Vaporize to gas"]
    N6["Gas phase"]
    N7["Ionize to plasma"]
    N8["Plasma phase"]
    N9["Cool: reverse transitions"]
    N10["Equilibrium phase (P,T)"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|heat| N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N2 -->|cool| N9
    N9 -->|reverse| N1
    N8 --> N10
    N6 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
description: The states of matter form a transition diagram driven by temperature and pressure: adding energy carries a substance from solid to liquid to gas and then to plasma through melting, vaporization, and ionization, while removing energy reverses these transitions; the equilibrium phase is fixed by position on the pressure-temperature diagram.

sources.title: Atkins' Physical Chemistry (11th ed.)
sources.authors: Atkins, P.; de Paula, J.; Keeler, J.

sources.title: Introduction to Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
